primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, en comptes consolidés, des modèles de comptabilité de couverture potentiellement applicables à une exposition de change liée à des dividendes intragroupe ou à la créance correspondante reconnue

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge-accounting eligibility, hedged-item and hedge-model questions, so IFRS 9 governs and IAS 39 is discarded for this issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is about hedge-accounting eligibility and models for foreign currency risk in consolidation, which are directly addressed by IFRS 9 and not by the residual IAS 39 portfolio fair value hedge exception.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern eligible hedged items in consolidation and define the named hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify how foreign exchange differences on monetary items and intragroup balances arise and are presented in consolidated financial statements.

peripheral_authority.reason: This Interpretation addresses hedges of net investments in foreign operations specifically, which is adjacent but not central to identifying peer approaches for intragroup dividends or receivables.
These paragraphs concern disclosures after hedge accounting is applied and do not determine the peer accounting models.

treatment_families.family: Named hedge accounting relationship types for foreign currency risk
Eligibility of intragroup items as hedged items in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including qualifying foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate named hedge accounting model and it remains a peer top-level hedge model within the governing literature.
